title: Incredibly Crispy Korean Sausage Jjigae
description: A funky, crispy, and comforting Korean dish featuring Italian fennel sausages, perfect for a sick day, made with a low-fat friendly approach in a gui form.
instructions: Heat the toasted sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Italian fennel sausages and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the sausages from the skillet and set aside.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the Gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, Gochugaru, and brown sugar to the skillet, whisking to combine.
Bring the sauce to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the kimchi to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the sausages to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top of each sausage.
Use a kitchen torch to flambe the sausages, or cook for an additional 2 minutes, until crispy and caramelized.
Garnish with green onions and serve hot over rice.
